original_hypothesis.title: WLpMoeMasterChef Reward Loss When First Wrapping with lpSupply Zero
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: In WLpMoeMasterChef's updateRewards modifier, when lpSupply == 0 (no LP deposited yet), the pidAccRewardPerShares_e18 is not incremented even if rewards are distributed during the MasterChef deposit call. Any rewards distributed by MasterChef during the first deposit are permanently lost since they cannot be attributed to any LP holder.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The updateRewards modifier reads lpSupply before the body (deposit) executes. For the first deposit, lpSupply == 0. After the body executes (deposit to MasterChef), reward tokens may have been distributed. The post-body loop checks `if (lpSupply != 0)` which is false for the first deposit, so rewards are never added to pidAccRewardPerShares_e18.

proof_of_signal.pattern: updateRewards modifier: line 62 `lpSupply = IMasterChef(MASTER_CHEF).getDeposit(pid, address(this))` (0 before first wrap); post-body line 71: `if (lpSupply != 0)` — false for first deposit, reward increment skipped

preconditions: First wrap call for a specific pid
MasterChef distributes rewards during the deposit call

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Reward accounting uses pre-deposit lpSupply which is 0 for first deposit
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Rewards distributed by MasterChef during first deposit are stranded in the contract
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Should use post-deposit lpSupply or handle lpSupply == 0 edge case

analysis_questions: How much reward does MasterChef typically distribute during a deposit call?
Are stranded rewards permanently locked or do they get attributed on next deposit?
Can an attacker front-run to be the first depositor and then claim disproportionate rewards?
validation_requirements: Test first wrap for a pid with active reward distribution
Verify reward token balance increase in contract vs pidAccRewardPerShares update
Check if stranded rewards are eventually distributed to subsequent depositors

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: First user wraps LP for a pid. MasterChef distributes MOE rewards during the deposit. Those rewards are received by WLpMoeMasterChef but pidAccRewardPerShares_e18 is not updated (lpSupply was 0). Rewards remain in the contract. On the next deposit/withdrawal, the accumulated rewards are attributed to the current lpSupply, benefiting the second depositor disproportionately.
prerequisites: Being the first to wrap LP for a specific MasterChef pid

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Small reward token loss per first deposit per pid; accumulated rewards may benefit later depositors
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: First depositor loses their proportional rewards
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Lost rewards remain in contract and may be distributed to later users

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for first wrap behavior with reward distribution

technical_investigation_focus.execution_timing: Only on first deposit per pid
technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Reward token balance discrepancy vs accounted rewards

validation_result.mechanism: reward accounting gap when lpSupply is zero
validation_result.impact_summary: When lpSupply == 0 during updateRewards, any reward tokens received during the body (deposit/withdraw/claim) are permanently stranded in the contract, never attributed to any LP holder. Practical impact is very low under standard MasterChef behavior since first deposits yield 0 pending rewards.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The updateRewards modifier in WLpMoeMasterChef captures `lpSupply` BEFORE the function body executes. When `lpSupply == 0`, the post-body loop skips the `pidAccRewardPerShares_e18` increment. This means any reward tokens received between the pre-body and post-body parts are never attributed to any LP holder.

However, the practical impact hinges on whether MasterChef actually distributes rewards during a deposit when the depositor has 0 prior stake. In standard Merchant Moe MasterChef implementations, `deposit` calculates `pending = oldBalance * accRewardPerShare / PRECISION - rewardDebt`. For a first deposit, `oldBalance = 0`, so `pending = 0` and no rewards are sent. This means the defect exists in code but is unlikely to cause actual reward loss under normal conditions.

Importantly, the stranded rewards are NOT redistributed to future depositors (contrary to the hypothesis's claim). On subsequent calls, `rewardBeforeAmts` captures the full contract balance (including stranded tokens), and only NEW reward deltas are attributed. The stranded rewards remain permanently locked in the contract.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof_of_signal correctly identifies the vulnerable code at WLpMoeMasterChef.sol lines 58-78. Line 62 reads `lpSupply = IMasterChef(MASTER_CHEF).getDeposit(_pid, address(this))` which returns 0 before first deposit. Line 71 checks `if (lpSupply != 0)` which is false for the first deposit, causing the reward per share increment to be skipped. The code pattern is a genuine defensive programming deficiency - it does not account for the possibility that rewards could arrive during the function body when lpSupply is 0.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: lpSupply is captured before the function body (e.g., deposit) executes. When lpSupply == 0, the post-body reward per share update is skipped entirely. Any reward tokens received during the body execution are permanently stranded in the contract, never reflected in pidAccRewardPerShares_e18.

This is called by wrap(). The updateRewards modifier captures lpSupply before the deposit executes. On first call for a pid, lpSupply is 0. The MasterChef.deposit() in the body may trigger reward distribution (unlikely for first deposit but possible with non-standard rewarders), which would then not be accounted for.

calling_sequence: 2. wrap validates the LP and receives tokens
5. updateRewards pre-body: lpSupply = getDeposit(pid, this) = 0 (first deposit)
7. Body: IMasterChef.deposit(pid, _amt) executes, may send rewards
8. updateRewards post-body: lpSupply == 0, so if-guard at line 73 is false
9. pidAccRewardPerShares_e18 is written but NOT incremented
10. Back in wrap: idAccRewardPerShares_e18[id] set to pidAccRewardPerShares_e18[pid] (unchanged)
11. Any rewards received in step 7 are now permanently stranded in contract
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The lpSupply variable is captured at WLpMoeMasterChef.sol:62 before the deposit executes. After the deposit at line 224 (inside _depositToMasterChef body), lpSupply is still the pre-deposit value (0 for first deposit). The post-body loop at line 71-77 checks `if (lpSupply != 0)` which evaluates to false, causing the reward per share increment to be skipped. The pidAccRewardPerShares_e18 is written at line 77 but with the unchanged value. On subsequent calls, rewardBeforeAmts will include the stranded balance, so the stranded tokens are never part of any future delta calculation either.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation:
The attack scenario described in the hypothesis is partially correct but has important caveats:

1. The code defect IS real: when lpSupply == 0, rewards received during the body are not accounted for.

2. However, the precondition 'MasterChef distributes rewards during the first deposit' is unlikely under standard MasterChef behavior. Standard MasterChef calculates pending rewards as `oldBalance * accRewardPerShare - rewardDebt`, and for a first deposit, oldBalance = 0 yielding pending = 0.

3. The claim that 'stranded rewards benefit the second depositor disproportionately' is INCORRECT. The stranded rewards remain in the contract balance but are never attributed through pidAccRewardPerShares_e18. On subsequent calls, they become part of rewardBeforeAmts and are excluded from the delta. They are permanently locked.

4. Possible (but unlikely) scenarios where this could cause actual loss: (a) non-standard extra rewarder that sends fixed rewards on any deposit, (b) direct token donations to the contract before first deposit followed by some mechanism that causes balance changes during deposit.
validation_result.impact_assessment:
LOW impact. While the code pattern IS a defect (failing to handle the edge case of rewards arriving when lpSupply == 0), the practical impact is minimal:

1. Standard MasterChef sends 0 rewards on first deposit → no actual loss
2. Even if loss occurred, it would be a one-time event per pid (only on first deposit when lpSupply transitions from 0 to non-zero)
3. The amount at risk would be limited to whatever rewards MasterChef sends during a single deposit call
4. Stranded rewards are permanently locked, not exploitable for profit by other users
5. No direct financial attack vector exists because the attacker cannot force MasterChef to distribute rewards when lpSupply is 0
recommended_mitigations: After the function body executes, re-read lpSupply from MasterChef instead of using the cached pre-body value: `uint lpSupplyAfter = IMasterChef(MASTER_CHEF).getDeposit(_pid, address(this));` and use lpSupplyAfter for the reward per share calculation
Alternatively, add a special case: if lpSupply was 0 and is now > 0, use the new lpSupply value for the reward calculation
Consider adding a sweep function (governor-only) to recover permanently stranded reward tokens
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy WLpMoeMasterChef with a mock MasterChef that sends a fixed reward on every deposit call (even when user has 0 prior stake). 2. Call wrap() for the first time for a specific pid. 3. Verify that reward tokens are received by WLpMoeMasterChef but pidAccRewardPerShares_e18 remains 0. 4. Call wrap() a second time with a different user. 5. Verify that the first user cannot claim the stranded rewards through harvest or unwrap. 6. Verify the stranded tokens remain permanently in the contract.
uncertainties: MasterChef and extra rewarder implementations are external contracts not available in scope - cannot definitively determine if rewards are distributed on first deposit
The extra rewarder (IMasterChefRewarder) behavior is unknown and might have non-standard reward distribution logic that could trigger the issue
The hypothesis claims rewards benefit later depositors, but analysis shows they are permanently stranded - the actual impact differs from the claimed impact
additional_testing_needed: Test with the actual deployed Merchant Moe MasterChef to verify if any rewards are distributed on first deposit
Test with various extra rewarder implementations to check if any send rewards regardless of prior stake
Test the full lifecycle: deposit-withdraw-deposit for the same pid to verify no rewards are stranded in the re-deposit scenario
Fuzz test the updateRewards modifier with various lpSupply values and reward amounts
